title: Where will 2026 rank among the hottest years on record?
description:
This market will resolve according to the numerical rank of how hot 2026 is when compared against all other years for which the Global Land-Ocean Temperature Index has data.

Years will be ranked in descending order, starting with the hottest as number 1, the second hottest as number 2, etc.

If 2026 ties with any other year, it will resolve according to the place the year it ties with occupies.

This market will resolve immediately once the specified data becomes available, regardless of whether the figure for the relevant years is later revised.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the figure found in the table titled "Land-Ocean Temperature Index (C)" under the column "No_Smoothing" in the row "2026" (https://data.giss.nasa.gov/gistemp/graphs/graph_data/Global_Mean_Estimates_based_on_Land_and_Ocean_Data/graph.txt). If NASA's "Global Temperature Index" is rendered permanently unavailable, other information from NASA may be used. If no information for 2026 is provided by NASA by March 1, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions 2026 as the second-warmest year on record, with market-implied odds of 62% for second place and 30.5% for first, reflecting the long-term anthropogenic warming trend overlaid by an emerging El Niño. Official data show 2025 as the third-warmest year, 0.08–0.12 °C cooler than 2024’s record, while early-2026 global anomalies have run modestly below 2024 levels. Developing El Niño conditions, now showing Niño 3.4 anomalies above +0.8 °C with an 82% probability of establishment by mid-2026, are expected to add warmth through the Northern Hemisphere winter. WMO and agency outlooks indicate an 86% chance that at least one year from 2026–2030 will exceed 2024, yet model spreads and current observations support the market’s lean toward a near-record but not record-setting outcome for 2026 itself.

title: Min Arctic sea ice extent this summer?
description:
This market will resolve according to the minimum Arctic sea ice extent for all days between August 1, 2026 and October 1, 2026, as published by the National Snow and Ice Data Center.

This market will remain open until data has been published for October 1, 2026, at which point it will resolve immediately. Any revisions to sea ice extent recorded after data is published for October 1, 2026 will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to thousands of square kilometers (e.g. 4.255 million sq km). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the National Snow and Ice Data Center, specifically the minimum value recorded for any day between August 1, 2026 and October 1, 2026 in the “NH-Daily-Extent” tab of the “Sea Ice Index Daily Extent” data set, available at https://nsidc.org/sea-ice-today/sea-ice-tools. If this resolution source becomes unavailable, another resolution source will be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: Record-low Arctic sea ice maximum in mid-March 2026, statistically tied with 2025 at approximately 14.29 million square kilometers according to NSIDC and NASA data, has provided an early head start to the melt season amid the long-term downward trend of roughly 12 percent per decade in September minima. Persistent below-average extents through April and into early June, driven by warmer Arctic temperatures and thinner ice, reinforce trader positioning toward outcomes below 4.2 million square kilometers. Official monitoring from NSIDC and JAXA shows 2026 tracking near or below recent low years, with model consensus and climatological baselines supporting elevated probabilities for a near-record minimum in September. Key upcoming factors include summer atmospheric patterns and updated NSIDC Sea Ice Index reports that could refine melt trajectories.

title: Measles cases in U.S. in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026 according to the CDC case counter between January 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter (see: https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) at the resolution time. If the counter becomes unavailable, another credible source will be used.

Note: Only cases reported by the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter will qualify, regardless of reports from U.S. State agencies or other sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Measles transmission in the United States remains elevated in 2026 due to sustained outbreaks fueled by international importations and spread within under-vaccinated communities.** As of early June, the CDC has confirmed 2,030 cases this year—already approaching or exceeding the 2,288 cases recorded for all of 2025—with 93 percent outbreak-linked across 40 jurisdictions. Measles, a highly contagious paramyxovirus with a basic reproduction number of 12–18, spreads rapidly where two-dose MMR coverage falls below the 95 percent herd-immunity threshold. Official surveillance shows most cases among children and young adults, consistent with gaps in routine immunization. Weekly CDC updates and state health department reports provide the primary data releases that can shift market-implied odds, alongside any revised elimination-status assessment later in the year.

title: 9.0 or above earthquake before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if 1 or more earthquakes with a magnitude of 9.0 or higher occur anywhere on Earth between December 8, 2025 12:00 PM ET, and December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Hazards Program (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/browse/significant.php#sigdef).

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used. 

After a qualifying earthquake is registered, this market will remain open for 24 hours to account for any revisions to its recorded magnitude. After 24 hours, this market will resolve according to the latest provided data.

eventMetadata.context_description: The market-implied 93.5% probability of no 9.0-or-greater earthquake by the end of 2026 reflects the extreme rarity of such megathrust events, which require simultaneous rupture across hundreds of kilometers of subduction-zone fault lines capable of storing and releasing enormous strain energy. Global records from the USGS and historical catalogs show only a handful of magnitude 9+ quakes in the past century, typically separated by decades and concentrated in specific tectonic settings such as the Pacific Ring of Fire. While continuous seismic monitoring could detect precursory foreshock sequences or accelerated strain, current models indicate low likelihood of an event reaching the required threshold within the remaining timeframe. An unexpected full-margin rupture in a high-strain subduction zone remains the primary, albeit low-probability, pathway that could shift sentiment.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 95.5% market-implied probability for no magnitude 10.0 or larger earthquake before 2027 stems primarily from established geophysical limits documented by the U.S. Geological Survey. Earthquake magnitude scales directly with fault rupture length, and no known fault segment is long enough to generate a 10.0 event without spanning much of the planet's circumference. The historical maximum remains 9.5, recorded in Chile in 1960 on a roughly 1,000-mile fault. Continuous USGS seismic monitoring through 2026 shows typical large events in the 7–9 range, with no observational data or model revisions suggesting a shift. While measurement uncertainties exist in early records, the physical constraints make a 10.0 outcome before the 2027 resolution threshold highly improbable.

title: Major meteor strike (10kt+) in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a natural meteoroid (bolide) explodes in Earth's atmosphere with a total impact energy greater than or equal to 10 kilotons of TNT equivalent between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The object must be classified as a natural meteoroid; events involving artificial objects or reentry vehicles do not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be the NASA JPL Fireball and Bolide Data repository: https://cneos.jpl.nasa.gov/fireballs/. The relevant field for determining impact energy is the “Impact Energy (kt)” column. If this dataset has not been updated to include all relevant dates by February 28, 2027, or if the NASA JPL Fireball and Bolide Data repository becomes permanently unavailable, this market may resolve based on a consensus of credible sources including the European Space Agency (ESA), the International Asteroid Warning Network (IAWN), the U.S. Department of Defense, or credible reporting of a scientific consensus, such as a NASA press release.

If this dataset has not been updated to include all relevant events by March 31, 2027, or if the Smithsonian GVP becomes permanently unavailable, this market may resolve based on a consensus of credible scientific sources, including the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS), national or regional volcanic observatories, or credible reporting of a scientific consensus.

Note: Smithsonian Institution Global Volcanism Program databases, which include eruptions that reached the relevant threshold prior to this market’s timeframe (e.g., https://volcano.si.edu/faq/index.cfm?question=eruptionsbyyear&checkyear=2025), will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: No VEI ≥4 eruptions have been confirmed through mid-2026 according to Smithsonian Global Volcanism Program records, aligning with the market's 66% implied probability for zero such events. Large explosive eruptions of this scale remain infrequent, with global averages historically around one every few years and significant year-to-year variability driven by magma chamber dynamics and tectonic settings. Ongoing activity at volcanoes like Fuego and Dukono has produced notable explosions but lacks evidence of reaching VEI 4 thresholds based on plume heights or ejecta volumes. Traders appear to weigh the remaining six months against this baseline rarity and the absence of new unrest signals that could escalate intensity.

title: 1 megaton meteor strike in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a natural meteoroid (bolide) explodes in Earth's atmosphere with a total impact energy greater than or equal to 1 megaton (1000 kilotons) of TNT equivalent between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The object must be classified as a natural meteoroid; events involving artificial objects or reentry vehicles do not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be the NASA JPL Fireball and Bolide Data repository: https://cneos.jpl.nasa.gov/fireballs/. The relevant field for determining impact energy is the “Impact Energy (kt)” column. If this dataset has not been updated to include all relevant dates by February 28, 2027, or if the NASA JPL Fireball and Bolide Data repository becomes permanently unavailable, this market may resolve based on a consensus of credible sources including the European Space Agency (ESA), the International Asteroid Warning Network (IAWN), the U.S. Department of Defense, or credible reporting of a scientific consensus, such as a NASA press release.

eventMetadata.context_description: NASA’s Planetary Defense Coordination Office and global surveys have cataloged the large majority of near-Earth objects capable of delivering 1-megaton impact energy, with no confirmed trajectories intersecting Earth in 2026. This exhaustive monitoring, combined with low historical frequencies of roughly one such event every several decades, underpins the market’s 97% implied probability for no strike. Ongoing optical and radar observations continue to refine orbits and rule out threats. A realistic shift would require discovery of a previously undetected object tens of meters across whose refined trajectory shows Earth impact before year-end, though statistical models assign this scenario very low odds.

title: Will any month of 2026 be the hottest on record?
description:
This market will resolve based on the data for the Global Land-Ocean Temperature Index for any month of 2026 versus the data points available for all years for the relevant month on record. If any month of 2026 is the hottest of that month for any year in record, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Note: If any month of 2026 is tied for hottest with the same month of another year, this market will resolve to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be the figures found in the table titled "GLOBAL Land-Ocean Temperature Index in 0.01 degrees Celsius" (https://data.giss.nasa.gov/gistemp/tabledata_v4/GLB.Ts+dSST.txt). If NASA's "Global Temperature Index" is rendered permanently unavailable, other information from NASA may be used.

This market may resolve immediately upon the release of data for any month which qualifies towards a "Yes" resolution.

If no information for all months is provided by NASA by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, a consensus of credible sources will be used to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing anthropogenic warming has pushed recent global temperatures to unprecedented levels, with 2024 marking the hottest year on record and 2025 ranking third-warmest according to NOAA and NASA analyses. The equatorial Pacific is transitioning from neutral conditions toward El Niño, with forecasters assigning an 82% probability of emergence by mid-2026 and high confidence it will persist through year-end, a pattern historically linked to elevated monthly anomalies. April 2026 already showed a 1.12°C departure above the long-term average, continuing the streak of top-ranked months. These factors underpin the market-implied 82% odds for a new monthly record sometime in 2026, though model variability in El Niño strength introduces some uncertainty around peak timing and intensity.

title: How many Tornadoes in the US in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of tornadoes recorded in the United States in 2026, based on the monthly counts published on the National Centers for Environmental Information U.S. Tornadoes Time Series page (see: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/monitoring/tornadoes/time-series).

Only tornadoes appearing in the final NCEI dataset for all months of 2026 will count.

As of market creation, the December report is not yet scheduled, however the release schedule can be found here: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/monitoring/dyk/monthly-releases. The market will resolve based on the first relevant tornado count published on the NCEI tornado time-series page after this scheduled release time.

If the value published after this scheduled release time is labeled preliminary, it will still determine resolution, and the market will resolve independently of any subsequent revisions, corrections, or retroactive adjustments. 

The market will not resolve based on any preliminary values published before the scheduled release time.

If no data is published by the scheduled release time, or if the NCEI website is temporarily unavailable, this market will remain open until that data is made available. If the relevant data is not made available by the date of the next scheduled publication ET, this market will resolve based on available data for the most recent prior month. If the NCEI website becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve using another credible source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Above-average tornado activity early in 2026 has driven strong trader consensus toward totals of 1,250 or more for the full year. NOAA's Storm Prediction Center reports roughly 692 preliminary tornadoes through early June, well ahead of the historical pace after an 80 percent above-average January–March period featuring multiple outbreaks. Peak season months remain ahead, and recent model guidance plus climatological analogs support continued elevated risk amid favorable atmospheric conditions. Official counts will undergo verification, but current observational trends and the heavy weighting of spring activity explain why markets assign over three-quarters probability to the highest bin.

title: How many 7.0 or above earthquakes by June 30? (Higher Strikes)
description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of earthquakes with a magnitude of 7.0 or higher that occur anywhere on Earth between December 4, 2025, 12:00 PM ET, and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Hazards Program (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/browse/significant.php#sigdef).

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until July 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The market's emphasis on eight or fewer magnitude 7.0+ earthquakes by June 30 reflects a year-to-date total of roughly seven such events through early June, capped by the June 8 magnitude 7.8 offshore the Philippines, according to USGS data. This pace aligns closely with the long-term global average of 15–16 events annually, or about seven to eight expected in the first half of the year along active subduction zones of the Pacific Ring of Fire. Natural week-to-week variability in seismic release means the final three weeks could add zero to three more quakes without deviating from historical patterns, keeping the ≤8 outcome as the trader consensus while higher counts remain possible if activity clusters before resolution.

title: Magnitude 6.5+ earthquake in LA before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if an earthquake with a magnitude of 6.5 or greater occurs within a 50-mile radius of Los Angeles, California (34.0522° N, 118.2437° W) between market creation and December 31, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Map (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/map/?extent=32.54681,-121.05835&extent=35.63944,-114.90601).

For the purposes of this market, The epicenter must fall within 50 miles of Los Angeles (34.0522° N, 118.2437° W), calculated as a straight-line distance from the epicenter to the reference point.

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until January 7, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The brief window until 2027 underpins the 92.5% market-implied probability against a magnitude 6.5 or greater earthquake striking the Los Angeles region, consistent with USGS long-term models indicating roughly 60% odds of a magnitude 6.7 event over the next 30 years. Recent seismic monitoring by the USGS and Southern California Earthquake Center reveals only low-magnitude activity with no sustained swarms or notable strain accumulation on key faults such as the San Andreas or local thrust systems, aligning with historical recurrence intervals of several decades between events of this scale on the Newport-Inglewood, Puente Hills, or Hollywood faults. Trader consensus reflects these baseline rates and the absence of precursors that would elevate near-term hazard. New GPS or seismometer data revising fault slip rates or an unexpected foreshock sequence could alter assessments, though such shifts remain statistically uncommon in the immediate term.

title: Vesuvius eruption with 1+ VEI in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Vesuvius volcano erupts with a Volcanic Explosivity Index (VEI) of 1 or greater between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be the Smithsonian Institution Global Volcanism Program (GVP: https://volcano.si.edu/), including the 2026 eruptions page (https://volcano.si.edu/faq/index.cfm?question=eruptionsbyyear&checkyear=2026).

This market will resolve as soon as a qualifying eruption occurs, or once December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET has passed and no qualifying eruption has occurred. If there is an ongoing Vesuvius eruption at that time, this market may remain open for an additional 14 calendar days to verify the VEI rating of the ongoing eruption.

If the Smithsonian GVP becomes permanently unavailable, or if an ongoing eruption has not been assigned a VEI rating within 14 calendar days of December 31, 2026, this market may resolve based on a consensus of credible scientific sources, including the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS), national or regional volcanic observatories, or credible reporting of a scientific consensus.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mount Vesuvius remains in a prolonged quiescent phase with a green alert level, showing only low-level seismic activity and minor hydrothermal degassing according to INGV monitoring. No significant precursors such as increased seismicity, ground deformation, or elevated gas emissions have been detected that would indicate magma ascent capable of producing a VEI 1+ eruption in 2026. This aligns with the volcano’s historical pattern of multi-decade repose intervals following its last eruption in 1944. Trader consensus at 92.5% “No” reflects these stable observations and the low annual probability of unrest at closed-conduit stratovolcanoes. A sudden shift in monitoring data or an unexpected phreatic event could alter odds, though current indicators make such developments improbable before year-end.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mount Etna entered 2026 with a flank eruption beginning January 1, featuring lava flows from a fissure in Valle del Bove and minor ash emissions monitored by Italy’s National Institute of Geophysics and Volcanology. This activity aligns with the volcano’s pattern of frequent Strombolian and effusive episodes that typically register VEI 1 or lower, rather than the moderate explosive scale of VEI 2+. No escalation to stronger ash plumes or higher explosivity has occurred through mid-year, and current seismic and gas data show steady but contained magma movement. Traders weigh this against Etna’s historical record of variable intensity, where VEI 2+ events remain less common amid mostly low-level output, supporting the 58.5% implied probability of no such eruption for the full year.

title: 2026 May 1st, 2nd, 3rd hottest on record?
description:
This market will resolve based on the data for the Global Land-Ocean Temperature Index for May 2026 versus the data points available for all other Mays on record. 

Note: If May 2026 is tied for first, second, or third hottest with another year, it will qualify for the bracket it ties with.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the figures found in the table titled "GLOBAL Land-Ocean Temperature Index in 0.01 degrees Celsius" under the column "May" (https://data.giss.nasa.gov/gistemp/tabledata_v4/GLB.Ts+dSST.txt). If NASA's "Global Temperature Index" is rendered permanently unavailable, other information from NASA may be used.
If no information for May 2026 is provided by NASA by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, a consensus of credible sources will be used to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Copernicus Climate Change Service data confirm May 2026 ranked as the second-warmest May globally on record, driven by the long-term anthropogenic warming trend superimposed on a developing El Niño that elevated land and ocean surface temperatures above the 1991-2020 baseline. This positions the month behind only the 2024 peak while comfortably ahead of prior years, aligning with the market's 99.5% implied probability for second place. Historical analogs show such rankings stabilize once multiple agency datasets converge, though minor revisions in final NOAA or NASA processing or unexpected late-month cooling in key regions could theoretically shift the precise order within the top three.

title: May 2026 Temperature Increase (ºC)
description:
This market will resolve according to the value reported by the Global Land-Ocean Temperature Index for May 2026 when it is released.

An anomaly within a named bracket for May 2026 is necessary and sufficient to resolve this market immediately once the data becomes available, regardless of whether the figure for May 2026 is later revised.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the figure found in the table titled "GLOBAL Land-Ocean Temperature Index in 0.01 degrees Celsius" under the column "May" in the row "2026" (https://data.giss.nasa.gov/gistemp/tabledata_v4/GLB.Ts+dSST.txt).

If NASA’s “Global Temperature Index” is rendered permanently unavailable, other information from NASA may be used.
If no information for May 2026 is provided by NASA by July 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent NOAA and Copernicus analyses show April and February 2026 global surface temperatures at 1.12°C and 1.18°C above the 20th-century average, respectively, with January near 1.47°C above the 1850–1900 baseline. These readings, combined with ENSO-neutral conditions transitioning toward a weak El Niño, anchor trader consensus at 97.7% implied probability for the 1.10–1.14°C bin in May 2026. Weak La Niña remnants earlier in the year contributed to modest cooling from 2023–2024 peaks while long-term greenhouse forcing maintains the elevated baseline. Official May data releases and any final model adjustments represent the main variables that could shift the narrow range.

title: Hantavirus pandemic in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the World Health Organization explicitly characterizes Hantavirus, Hantavirus Pulmonary Syndrome (HPS), Hemorrhagic Fever with Renal Syndrome (HFRS), or a Hantavirus-related outbreak as a "pandemic" in an official public communication between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An explicit characterization includes official WHO statements, reports, press briefings, or publications that clearly describe the outbreak as a "pandemic." A Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC) alone will not qualify unless it is also described as a pandemic.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official WHO communications. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent limited transmission of Andes virus during a May 2026 cruise ship cluster, with only eight to eleven confirmed cases and three deaths reported by WHO, underpins the 94.5% market-implied probability against a hantavirus pandemic in 2026. Official assessments from CDC and WHO classify global public health risk as low, citing inefficient person-to-person spread that requires prolonged close contact and occurs rarely even for this strain. Most hantaviruses remain rodent-borne with no sustained human chains, consistent with historical annual global incidence of 10,000–100,000 cases that stay geographically contained. Surveillance continues through summer 2026, but absent major evolutionary shifts enabling efficient airborne spread, current epidemiological data support trader consensus on limited outbreak potential.

title: Hantavirus vaccine in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any vaccine intended for humans and inoculating against Hantavirus (including Hantavirus Pulmonary Syndrome (HPS) or Hemorrhagic Fever with Renal Syndrome (HFRS)) receives full approval from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the FDA, including its list of approved vaccines (https://www.fda.gov/vaccines-blood-biologics/vaccines/vaccines-licensed-use-united-states), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: No licensed orthohantavirus vaccine is approved for general use in the United States or most countries as of mid-2026, with only the inactivated Hantavax available in South Korea for Hantaan virus–associated hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome. Multiple candidates, including DNA vaccines targeting Gn/Gc glycoproteins, mRNA platforms, protein subunits, and viral vectors, remain in preclinical stages or have completed only small Phase 1 trials showing neutralizing antibody responses but requiring multiple doses. A 2026 Andes virus outbreak on a cruise ship prompted renewed research interest and some early-stage advancement, yet regulatory pathways, manufacturing scale-up, and the viruses’ rarity continue to limit rapid progress toward approval within the year.

title: Hantavirus outbreak by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the World Health Organization (WHO) officially declares the hantavirus (or any strain or outbreak of the hantavirus) a Public Health Emergency of International Concern by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only a determination that the hantavirus is a Public Health Emergency of International Concern will qualify. Other designations will not be considered.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the WHO; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

tags.label: Public Health Emergency of International Concern

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus at 98.2% against a hantavirus outbreak by June 30 reflects the highly contained nature of the May 2026 Andes virus cluster linked to the MV Hondius cruise ship. Official reports from WHO and CDC document roughly 11–13 total cases (mostly laboratory-confirmed), with transmission limited to close shipboard contact and no sustained community spread. No Andes virus cases have been confirmed in the United States despite monitoring of exposed individuals across multiple states, consistent with the virus’s typical rodent-borne, non-person-to-person pattern outside rare exceptions. Historical U.S. surveillance shows only sporadic hantavirus pulmonary syndrome cases annually, with no precedent for rapid escalation from such a small imported cluster within weeks. While continued contact tracing through late June could theoretically reveal additional linked infections, current epidemiological indicators and low baseline incidence make significant expansion before month-end highly improbable.

title: Hantavirus lab leak confirmed by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any hantavirus case linked to the hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship is confirmed to have originated from a medical laboratory or research facility by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Confirmation that the hantavirus case originated from a medical laboratory, research facility, diagnostic facility, or other controlled laboratory setting is required. Definitive confirmation that the lab-originated case directly caused the outbreak on the MV Hondius is not required, provided a consensus of credible reporting indicates that the lab-originated case is a possible source of, or is otherwise plausibly connected to, the MV Hondius outbreak.

Investigations, speculation, statements that a lab origin is possible or has not been ruled out, or statements unverified by a consensus of credible reporting will not suffice.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Official reports from WHO and CDC emphasize person-to-person transmission in a contained cluster, echoing historical patterns for this pathogen and showing no precursor signals of a lab incident or whistleblower developments in the past month. This narrative dominance, backed by rapid containment and expert consensus, mirrors how entertainment markets lock in frontrunners after decisive guild or critics' nods. A realistic upset would require an unexpected late-breaking investigation or verified sample mishandling to surface in the narrow remaining window, though current momentum makes such a plot twist improbable.

title: AI data center in space by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any orbital data center is successfully launched by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Orbital data center” refers to any spacecraft, satellite, or equivalent technology carrying computing infrastructure that is launched into Earth’s orbit for the purpose of providing data-center, cloud-computing, or artificial intelligence computing services and which includes at least 100 data-center-grade AI accelerators, GPUs, TPUs, or substantially equivalent compute processors, (e.g. NVIDIA H100 GPUs, Google TPUs, or equivalent or successor chips).

“Successfully launched” refers to any launch which successfully places a qualifying orbital data center into Earth’s orbit.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: AI data center in space by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any orbital data center is successfully launched by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Orbital data center” refers to any spacecraft, satellite, or equivalent technology carrying computing infrastructure that is launched into Earth’s orbit for the purpose of providing data-center, cloud-computing, or artificial intelligence computing services and which includes at least 100 data-center-grade AI accelerators, GPUs, TPUs, or substantially equivalent compute processors, (e.g. NVIDIA H100 GPUs, Google TPUs, or equivalent or successor chips).

“Successfully launched” refers to any launch which successfully places a qualifying orbital data center into Earth’s orbit.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent NVIDIA announcements advancing specialized hardware for orbital data centers, including the Space-1 Vera Rubin Module delivering up to 25x more AI compute than prior GPUs, are shaping trader views on near-term feasibility. Companies like SpaceX have detailed plans for satellites offering 150kW peak AI processing, while Nvidia-backed Starcloud has already deployed an initial AI-equipped satellite. Earth-based constraints around power consumption, permitting delays, and community opposition for large AI facilities are accelerating interest in space alternatives that leverage solar power and passive cooling. Key upcoming catalysts include further satellite launches, demonstrations of orbital inference workloads, and regulatory approvals for expanded constellations, though challenges in data transmission latency and maintenance remain unresolved.

title: Ebola pandemic in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the World Health Organization explicitly characterizes Ebola virus disease, Ebola, any Ebola strain, or any outbreak of ebola as a “pandemic” in an official public communication between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An explicit characterization includes official WHO statements, reports, press briefings, or publications that clearly describe the outbreak as a “pandemic.” A Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC) alone will not qualify unless it is also described as a pandemic.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official WHO communications. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Ebola pandemic in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the World Health Organization explicitly characterizes Ebola virus disease, Ebola, any Ebola strain, or any outbreak of ebola as a “pandemic” in an official public communication between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An explicit characterization includes official WHO statements, reports, press briefings, or publications that clearly describe the outbreak as a “pandemic.” A Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC) alone will not qualify unless it is also described as a pandemic.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official WHO communications. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The ongoing Bundibugyo virus Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda, declared a public health emergency of international concern by the WHO in May 2026 but explicitly not a pandemic emergency, drives the 92.5% market-implied probability against a global pandemic this year. As of early June, official tallies show hundreds of confirmed cases concentrated in Ituri Province and limited spillover to Kampala, with no sustained community transmission beyond close contacts or healthcare settings and zero imported cases in the United States. CDC and WHO assessments emphasize low risk to distant populations due to the virus’s transmission dynamics, rapid contact tracing, and established response protocols honed from prior regional outbreaks. Historical patterns of Ebola containment further reinforce trader consensus. A realistic challenge would require rapid expansion into multiple new urban centers or countries with overwhelmed surveillance, though current model consensus and containment measures make this unlikely before year-end.

title: Ebola case in the US by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a confirmed case of Ebola in the territory of the United States of America reported between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Any active laboratory-confirmed Ebola infection identified within U.S. territory will qualify, regardless of where exposure, symptom onset, or testing occurred.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official government information (e.g., the CDC); however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

markets.question: Ebola case in the US by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a confirmed case of Ebola in the territory of the United States of America reported between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Any active laboratory-confirmed Ebola infection identified within U.S. territory will qualify, regardless of where exposure, symptom onset, or testing occurred.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official government information (e.g., the CDC); however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Strict U.S. entry screening, travel restrictions, and enhanced public health measures implemented since mid-May have kept the importation risk low during the ongoing Bundibugyo virus outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. As of early June, CDC and WHO reports confirm zero Ebola cases in the United States, with the overall risk to the American public assessed as low despite rising confirmed cases and deaths in affected African regions. Official surveillance, airport protocols at designated ports of entry, and rapid international containment efforts further reduce the chance of an undetected case arriving before the June 30 resolution date. Trader consensus at 89.5% for “No” aligns with these epidemiological controls and the short remaining window for any undetected transmission.

title: Highest temperature in Jinan on May 20?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Jinan Yaoqiang International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 20 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Jinan Yaoqiang International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/jinan/ZSJN.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

title: Which countries will have an Ebola case in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a confirmed case of Ebola in the territory of the specified country reported between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Any active laboratory-confirmed, or otherwise officially confirmed, Ebola infection identified within the territory of the specified country will qualify, regardless of where exposure, symptom onset, or testing occurred.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from the relevant national and international health authorities; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: The ongoing Ebola outbreak caused by Bundibugyo virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda, declared a public health emergency of international concern by the WHO in May 2026, drives current trader focus on potential spread to additional countries through the remainder of the year. As of early June, DRC health authorities report hundreds of confirmed cases concentrated in Ituri and neighboring provinces, with Uganda confirming cases in Kampala linked to travel from the DRC. Neighboring nations sharing porous borders face elevated risk due to population movement, while enhanced surveillance by the CDC, WHO, and national ministries, alongside ring vaccination and contact tracing, could limit geographic expansion. Historical patterns show Ebola outbreaks often remain regionally contained but can cross borders when detection is delayed; upcoming WHO situation reports and case threshold updates will provide key signals for market resolution.

title: June 2026 Temperature Increase (ºC)
description:
This market will resolve according to the value reported by the Global Land-Ocean Temperature Index for June 2026 when it is released.

An anomaly within a named bracket for June 2026 is necessary and sufficient to resolve this market immediately once the data becomes available, regardless of whether the figure for June 2026 is later revised.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the figure found in the table titled "GLOBAL Land-Ocean Temperature Index in 0.01 degrees Celsius" under the column "Jun" in the row "2026" (https://data.giss.nasa.gov/gistemp/tabledata_v4/GLB.Ts+dSST.txt).

If NASA’s “Global Temperature Index” is rendered permanently unavailable, other information from NASA may be used.
If no information for June 2026 is provided by NASA by August 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Emerging El Niño conditions in the equatorial Pacific represent the primary driver behind current market-implied odds favoring a June 2026 global temperature anomaly of 1.10–1.19°C. NOAA and IRI forecasts show an 82–98% probability of El Niño onset during May–July 2026, following neutral-to-warm sea surface temperatures that have already produced weekly Niño 3.4 anomalies near +0.9°C. This developing warming typically amplifies global near-surface temperatures by 0.1–0.2°C above baseline within months, aligning with recent monthly observations such as February 2026 at 1.18°C and 2025’s annual average near 1.19°C. Trader consensus reflects model uncertainty in El Niño strength and timing, with lower-probability bins above 1.20°C awaiting stronger ocean-atmosphere coupling or revised June data releases.

title: 2026 June 1st, 2nd, 3rd hottest on record?
description:
This market will resolve based on the data for the Global Land-Ocean Temperature Index for June 2026 versus the data points available for all other Junes on record. 

Note: If June 2026 is tied for first, second, or third hottest with another year, it will qualify for the bracket it ties with.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the figures found in the table titled "GLOBAL Land-Ocean Temperature Index in 0.01 degrees Celsius" under the column "Jun" (https://data.giss.nasa.gov/gistemp/tabledata_v4/GLB.Ts+dSST.txt). If NASA's "Global Temperature Index" is rendered permanently unavailable, other information from NASA may be used.
If no information for June 2026 is provided by NASA by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, a consensus of credible sources will be used to resolve this market.

title: Measles cases in U.S. by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026, according to the CDC case counter by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter 'Total Cases' in 2026 (see: https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) at the resolution time. If the counter becomes unavailable, another credible source will be used.

Note: Only cases reported by the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter will qualify, regardless of reports from U.S. State agencies or other sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing measles transmission across 40 U.S. jurisdictions has driven 2,030 confirmed cases through June 4, 2026, according to CDC surveillance, already approaching the 2,288 total recorded for all of 2025. Ninety-three percent of cases link to outbreaks, with 92 percent occurring in unvaccinated or unknown-status individuals and the majority among children and adolescents. Official counts reflect laboratory-confirmed reports, which can lag slightly behind symptom onset, while new importations and local spread in under-vaccinated communities continue to fuel growth. Weekly CDC updates and any shifts in state-level reporting through late June will determine the final cumulative figure by month-end.

title: Precipitation in NYC in June?
description:
This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in inches in Central Park, New York City between June 1 and June 30, 2026, 11:59PM ET according to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be NOAA, specifically the figure for June 2026 when the "Monthly summarized data" for "Central Park NY" is selected with the variable set to "Precipitation" at the https://www.weather.gov/wrh/climate?wfo=okx link once that figure is finalized for the whole month of June 2026.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 2 decimal places (e.g., 1.54), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The closely matched probabilities around 50–80 mm reflect uncertainty following an unusually wet early June, with parts of southern England already accumulating near a full month's typical rainfall amid a strengthened jet stream steering Atlantic weather systems across the UK. Met Office outlooks indicate a slight tilt toward wetter-than-average summer conditions overall, though model runs diverge on whether high pressure will build later in the month to suppress further precipitation. Historical June averages near 45 mm provide baseline context, yet the current unsettled pattern and variable steering flows keep the 60–70 mm and 80 mm+ bins nearly even with lower bands, pending updated forecast runs and observational data through mid-month.

eventMetadata.context_description: The market's closely matched probabilities around 120-130 mm reflect Seoul's historical June average of roughly 130 mm, with traders balancing expectations for a typical early-summer pattern against variability from the impending Jangma monsoon. Early June has featured limited rainfall under relatively stable conditions, while forecasts point to increasing showers and potential heavier totals after mid-month as the monsoon arrives around June 20, driven by warmer sea surface temperatures and shifting pressure gradients. Official guidance from the Korea Meteorological Administration and ensemble models will refine intensity projections, with outcomes hinging on exact onset timing, storm frequency, and any deviations from climatological baselines.

eventMetadata.context_description: The market's tight clustering around 375-425 mm reflects the Hong Kong Observatory's late-May seasonal outlook for normal to below-normal June rainfall across the territory, set against above-normal temperatures. Early June observations show scattered showers and isolated thunderstorms rather than sustained monsoon convergence, aligning with typical southwest monsoon onset patterns and limited tropical cyclone activity so far. Historical June totals average 330-450 mm, modulated by the strength of the monsoon trough and steering flows that can deliver heavy episodes or suppress them. Model guidance continues to favor moderate accumulation unless later forecast runs indicate enhanced moisture transport or a nearby system, with the next official updates likely to sharpen resolution probabilities for the balance of the month.

title: What caused the Blue Origin New Glenn Explosion?
description:
On May 28, 2026, a Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded on its launch pad during a hot-fire test (see: https://www.cbsnews.com/news/blue-origin-new-glenn-rocket-explodes-launchpad-florida/).

This market will resolve according to the listed category that most directly aligns with the technical failure publicly identified by Blue Origin as the cause of this explosion.

If Blue Origin identifies multiple contributing causes, this market will resolve based on the cause Blue Origin describes as the primary cause of the explosion. If Blue Origin does not designate a primary cause, this market will resolve based on the cause most prominently featured in their official disclosure.

If Blue Origin identifies any cause not clearly encompassed by any of the listed options, this market will resolve to “Other.”

A preliminary or interim finding may qualify for resolution, provided it identifies a cause without material qualification.

If Blue Origin does not publicly disclose the primary cause of the explosion by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No Cause Announced by December 31.”

An overwhelming consensus of credible reporting (e.g. reporting on Blue Origin communications through the media or the results of governmental investigations) on the cause of the explosion may also qualify in place of a formal Blue Origin declaration.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Blue Origin; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The May 28 static-fire anomaly at Cape Canaveral, which destroyed a fueled New Glenn vehicle and heavily damaged Launch Complex 36 during a pre-launch hot-fire test, remains under active Blue Origin investigation with no official cause disclosed. Traders have priced a propellant leak highest at 42.5% due to visible vapor clouds and pressure-wave patterns in footage consistent with liquid methane or oxygen release igniting during engine start. Software or control-system issues rank next at 31.5% given the test's automated sequencing, while the 27.1% chance of no December 31 announcement reflects typical aerospace timelines for root-cause analysis and public reporting. Valve or plumbing failures trail at 23.0% as a plausible mechanical precursor, with engine or ground-support issues lower as they appear less aligned with initial visual evidence.

title: Blue Origin New Glenn launch in 2026?
description:
If any Blue Origin New Glenn rocket successfully launches from its launch pad by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Yes”. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

New Glenn rocket refers to any Blue Origin rocket publicly identified by Blue Origin as New Glenn, including any newly manufactured, rebuilt, replacement, or reused New Glenn vehicle.

The outcome of any launch will be corroborated by examining official video provided by Blue Origin, as well as secondary video feeds and/or written reports if necessary.

Any subsequent anomaly (e.g., explosion or failed ascent) after the initial liftoff will have no bearing on the outcome. Any name change or rebranding of New Glenn will have no bearing on this market’s resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent static fire testing on May 28 at Cape Canaveral’s Launch Complex 36 triggered an explosion that destroyed the third New Glenn booster and upper stage while heavily damaging the pad infrastructure—the vehicle’s only active launch site. Repair estimates exceed one year, shifting all subsequent missions to TBD status despite Blue Origin’s June statements pledging a return to flight before year-end. The April 19 NG-3 flight achieved first-stage reuse but ended in payload deployment anomalies, underscoring ongoing vehicle maturation. With the facility offline through at least mid-2027 and no alternate East Coast pad available, trader consensus assigns 84% probability to no New Glenn launch occurring in 2026.

title: Flu Hospitalization Rate Week 22, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the cumulative influenza-associated hospitalization rate per 100,000 population for the United States, as reported for the specified week.

If the recorded data falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be CDC FluView / FluSurv-NET (see: https://www.cdc.gov/fluview/index.html). If the FluSurv-NET hospitalization rate for the specified week is not released by 11:59 PM ET on the tenth calendar day following the date of the prior FluView weekly report release, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

Note: Only the CDC FluSurv-NET cumulative hospitalization rate per 100,000 population for the specified week will qualify, regardless of estimates, projections, state-level reports, or other influenza surveillance metrics published by the CDC or other sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: The dominant market-implied probability of 98.4% for an 85–90 flu hospitalization rate in Week 22, 2026 stems from established seasonal influenza patterns tracked by CDC surveillance systems. By late May, official data consistently show sharp post-peak declines driven by waning viral transmission, rising population immunity from winter circulation, and warmer weather reducing indoor spread. Historical baselines and epidemiological models place late-spring weekly rates in this narrow band absent unusual summer activity. Key upcoming resolution inputs include the next CDC FluView release and any revised laboratory-confirmed hospitalization counts, which could shift if an atypical variant emerges or surveillance adjustments occur, though such deviations remain rare based on decades of seasonal observations.

title: How many 6.5 or above earthquakes June 8 - June 14?
description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of earthquakes with a magnitude of 6.5 or higher that occur anywhere on Earth between June 8, 2026, 12:00 AM ET, and June 14, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Hazards Program (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/browse/significant.php#sigdef).

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used.

This market may not resolve until the timeframe of this market has concluded. If a qualifying earthquake has been recorded on the final day, this market may remain open for 24 hours to allow for revisions to the recorded magnitude. After 24 hours, this market will resolve according to the latest provided data.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 10 Jun '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tr/%C3%A7ubuk/LTAC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment centers on ensemble forecast models indicating a daytime high of 23–24 °C in Ankara, driven by a cooler-than-average northwesterly flow advecting maritime air from the Black Sea region. Moderate cloud cover and higher relative humidity are limiting surface radiative heating, while light winds reduce turbulent mixing that would otherwise enhance warming. With probabilities tightly clustered, small differences in model resolution of low-level moisture and timing of any afternoon clearing could shift the peak by 1 °C either way. Historical June climatology for the Anatolian plateau shows mean highs near 26 °C, underscoring how this synoptic setup deviates from typical seasonal conditions and keeps the market balanced between the 22 °C–or-below and 25 °C outcomes.

eventMetadata.context_description: The market's near-certain positioning on a 14°C daily maximum for Wellington on June 10 stems from official MetService forecasts showing stable winter conditions with a peak temperature aligning precisely to that threshold. In the Southern Hemisphere's cool season, typical highs in the capital range from 12–15°C under southerly flows and limited insolation, and current model runs from the New Zealand meteorological service confirm no warmer air mass or clear-sky heating will push readings higher. This consensus among traders reflects real-time observational data and short-range numerical weather prediction reliability on the final day of the event. A late surge above 14°C would require an unforecasted warm front or localized urban heat island effect exceeding standard measurement protocols, scenarios historical analogs indicate are improbable given the established synoptic pattern.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kuala Lumpur’s June climatology centers daily maxima near 32°C under the prevailing southwest monsoon regime, with Malaysian Meteorological Department guidance and numerical models showing no significant departure from this baseline for June 10. This places 32°C as the modal outcome, consistent with the narrow historical distribution where 32–33°C accounts for the large majority of June afternoons. The modest 18.5% implied probability on 33°C reflects the realistic chance of brief additional warming from reduced cloud cover or urban heat-island effects, while probabilities above 34°C remain negligible absent an atypical heat surge. Short-range forecasts issued in the preceding 48 hours have converged on this range, reinforcing trader consensus around the long-term average.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Masroor Airbase Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pk/karachi/OPKC.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current forecast models from regional meteorological agencies indicate a maximum temperature near 37°C for Karachi on June 10, consistent with early June climatology along the Arabian Sea coast where pre-monsoon conditions typically produce daily highs of 34–40°C. A persistent heat ridge over Sindh has maintained these levels without significant deviation in recent model runs, supporting the dominant market-implied probability for 37°C while limiting upside risk for 38°C or higher. Short-range numerical guidance shows minimal day-to-day shifts, though coastal humidity and any late steering adjustments could influence the final observed peak within the 36–38°C window.

title: Will it rain in Central Park on June 9?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any precipitation is recorded at the specified weather station on the specified date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in inches in Central Park, New York City on June 9, 2026 according to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).

The resolution source for this market will be NOAA, specifically the figure for the specified date when the “Monthly summarized data” for “Central Park NY” is selected with the variable set to “Precipitation” at the https://www.weather.gov/wrh/climate?wfo=okx link once that figure is finalized for the specified date.

Trace rain, specified by a “T” in the named column, will not qualify toward a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 2 decimal places (e.g., 1.54), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after data is finalized for this market’s timeframe will not be considered for this market’s resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The closely contested market odds around 50 percent for no rain in Central Park on June 9 stem from genuine uncertainty in localized precipitation data near the resolution threshold. Recent National Weather Service observations and model guidance for the New York metro area showed borderline moisture transport and weak convective cells, with radar estimates differing slightly from ground-based rain gauges on total accumulation. Key variables include exact storm track timing, urban heat island effects on rainfall distribution, and measurement precision across the park's microclimate. New official verification reports expected shortly from NOAA stations should clarify totals and allow decisive market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest National Weather Service and AccuWeather guidance for Miami on June 10 shows overnight lows in the upper 70s to low 80s Fahrenheit, driven by a warm, humid subtropical air mass with light easterly winds and limited nocturnal cooling under partly cloudy conditions. This aligns with early-June climatology where average lows hover near 76–78°F at Miami International Airport, but current model consensus favors slightly elevated readings amid ongoing southerly flow. Traders have concentrated implied probability on the 78–81°F bins because recent observations and short-range forecasts consistently point to these thresholds, with only minor downside risk if clearer skies develop overnight. The market will resolve on the official daily minimum recorded at the primary station.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for São Paulo’s June 10 high temperature centers on near-term meteorological model outputs and climatological baselines for the Southern Hemisphere winter. Official guidance and ensemble forecasts from agencies such as INMET and global numerical weather prediction systems indicate daytime maxima most likely between 22–25 °C under clear to partly cloudy skies with light winds, consistent with the city’s June average high near 22–23 °C. The tight clustering of market-implied odds around 24 °C (43.5 %) and 25 °C (35.5 %) reflects modest model spread and limited recent temperature anomalies, while lower probabilities for 26 °C or above capture the low likelihood of significant warm-air advection or reduced cloud cover on this date. Updated model runs and any late-morning surface observations remain the key near-term catalysts.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service forecasts for Miami on June 10 point to a daily high in the upper 80s under partly sunny skies with easterly winds and a 20% chance of afternoon thunderstorms, aligning with the closely matched market-implied odds for 86-87°F and 88-89°F. Typical early June climatology features average highs near 89°F at Miami International Airport, modulated by high humidity, sea-breeze effects, and convective activity that can cap peak temperatures. Recent model runs show limited spread, with any additional cloud cover or showers likely to keep readings below 90°F while historical analogs indicate occasional excursions into the low 90s remain possible if skies clear earlier than expected.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current National Weather Service and model guidance point to a warm air mass advecting into the Chicago area, supporting a daily high several degrees above the 79°F June 10 climatological normal. Ensemble forecasts cluster between 86°F and 93°F, with the tightest probability mass in the 90–93°F range reflecting modest uncertainty in boundary-layer mixing and afternoon cloud cover. Surface high pressure and southwesterly flow favor limited mixing and rapid daytime heating, while any scattered convection could cap temperatures slightly lower. Resolution hinges on the official maximum recorded at Chicago Midway or O’Hare by midnight local time, with updates from the National Weather Service expected through the evening.
